topic: Matched minimax EXCESS-LENGTH rate for distribution-free (conformal) coverage of a stochastic-intervention potential outcome Y^g in [0,1] under a heavy density-ratio tail, regime gamma0 in (1,2).

open_problems.open_problem: Establish the matched minimax high-probability excess-length rate, relative to oracle weighted conformal prediction, for training-conditionally valid distribution-free intervals for a stochastic-intervention potential outcome when the density ratio has tail index gamma0 in (1,2).

source_refs.claim: Schröder et al. give finite-sample conformal intervals for continuous-treatment potential outcomes with an estimated propensity, but do not characterize interval-length rates or a weak-overlap minimax frontier.
source_refs.author: Schröder, Frauen, Schweisthal, Heß, Melnychuk, Feuerriegel

source_refs.claim: Dorn proves asymptotic normality for thresholded doubly robust point estimation under weak overlap and identifies a nuisance-rate cost when the semiparametric bound is infinite, but does not study conformal prediction-set length.
source_refs.author: Dorn

source_refs.claim: Weighted conformal prediction gives distribution-free coverage under covariate shift when the likelihood ratio is known, but supplies no minimax efficiency or heavy-tail excess-length characterization for an estimated causal weight.
source_refs.author: Tibshirani, Barber, Candès, Ramdas

source_refs.excerpt: Conjecture 2: matching tail-localized lower bound (NOT YET PROVED)
the discharged core supports only a parametric lower floor plus estimator-side achievability

open_problems.why_unsolved: Exact weighted exchangeability gives coverage only for known weights, while the weak-overlap literature treats a clipped doubly robust scalar estimand rather than a weighted conformity-score quantile. The closest in-repo program likewise had an upper construction but only a parametric lower floor: its shell-local alternatives did not force the required loss. A converse here must additionally show that uncertainty confined to the thin high-w region compels enlargement of a connected, training-conditionally valid interval, rather than merely separating a scalar ATE.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A paired upper-and-lower theorem: a tail-aware cross-fit orthogonal clipped-WCP interval with high-probability oracle-relative excess length O(n^{-rho(gamma0,s,beta)}) and a Le Cam two-point or dyadic-shell construction forcing Omega(n^{-rho(gamma0,s,beta)}) excess length for every valid connected interval.

writing_exemplar.why: Its setup moves from continuous-treatment causal uncertainty to a covariate-shift representation and then to finite-sample conformal intervals.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: continuous-treatment decisions require uncertainty for Y(a) -> intervention induces propensity/distribution shift -> finite-sample causal conformal validity is possible but its weak-overlap length frontier is unknown

method_exemplars.technique: Use the weighted-exchangeability and weighted-quantile proof that converts a Radon--Nikodym ratio into an exact conformal coverage statement.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The stochastic-intervention law is a covariate-shift target law with density ratio w=g/pi, so its oracle interval is the weighted-conformal reference object.

method_exemplars.technique: Port thresholding under a tail condition and the orthogonal nuisance-remainder analysis for weak-overlap doubly robust scores.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Both problems localize error to rare low-propensity/high-weight observations; the excess-length upper bound needs the analogous clipping-versus-nuisance balance.

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt localized fuzzy hypotheses with divergence calibration and a separated causal target to a score-quantile/interval-length loss.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The archived gap was precisely the failure to turn a tail shell into an O(1)-divergent, target-separated lower-bound family.

open_problems.open_problem: At the critical density-ratio-tail boundary gamma=1, characterize the logarithmic oracle-excess-length penalty and determine whether training-conditionally distribution-free weighted-conformal intervals can adapt honestly over unknown gamma across the finite-variance boundary.

source_refs.excerpt: The v1 draft does not claim adaptivity over unknown (\kappa,\beta), uniform confidence intervals
The draft does not prove adaptivity over unknown \kappa, uniform confidence intervals

open_problems.why_unsolved: The archived weak-overlap work isolated the kappa=1 variance transition but expressly withdrew uniform confidence intervals and unknown-tail adaptivity. Near that boundary, clipping levels that are optimal on either side differ by logarithmic factors, whereas data-driven tail selection uses the same rare high-weight observations whose fluctuations invalidate finite-variance arguments. A pointwise tail calculation therefore cannot establish an honest adaptive length guarantee.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Either an honest adaptive weighted-conformal construction with a uniform high-probability oracle-excess-length bound containing the sharp gamma=1 logarithmic factor, or an impossibility theorem proving an unavoidable adaptation penalty for intervals valid simultaneously over tail classes straddling gamma=1.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the closest weak-overlap framing for explaining why an inverse-weight boundary changes inferential behavior.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: inverse-weight tails create nonregular behavior -> threshold choice controls the resulting bias and variation -> can a finite-sample valid prediction interval choose that threshold honestly at the critical tail boundary?

method_exemplars.technique: Use weak-overlap threshold selection and doubly robust asymptotic expansion with explicit tail moments to derive the boundary log term.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It separates a fixed-tail assertion from the tail-dependent threshold choice that must be made adaptive here.

method_exemplars.technique: Use localized alternatives calibrated to make adjacent difficulty regimes statistically indistinguishable.
method_exemplars.why_closest: An adaptation lower bound needs tail exponents near one whose oracle interval scales differ but whose samples cannot reliably select the correct clipping regime.

open_problems.open_problem: Give a class-uniform tail-envelope theorem for clipped weighted-conformal excess length under a specified density-ratio shell condition, so that its optimized upper rate is genuinely uniform over W(gamma,s,beta) rather than a law-by-law surrogate.

source_refs.excerpt: the proof yields law-specific comparison constants, not a class-uniform U_n \asymp R_n statement
only the class-uniform comparison and the shell-matching converse are open

open_problems.why_unsolved: The archived weak-overlap claim failed because its tail-shell constants existed separately for each law, so the clipping surrogate was not uniformly comparable to the actual envelope. The same obstruction is decisive for a conformal minimax result: a tail inequality can control one law's clipped weights while failing to give simultaneous constants for oracle-relative length and nuisance-induced score perturbations across the class.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sufficient-condition theorem imposing uniform shell regularity (or an equivalent tail-quantile condition) and proving c1 R_WCP(b) <= U_WCP(b) <= c2 R_WCP(b) uniformly over W(gamma,s,beta), followed by optimization over the clipping level.

writing_exemplar.why: Its presentation makes the weak-overlap threshold and nuisance/tail bookkeeping visible before stating an inference consequence.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: unbounded inverse weights invalidate regular-overlap expansions -> thresholding introduces a bias-variance-nuisance tradeoff -> which uniform tail condition makes that tradeoff a minimax conformal-length law?

method_exemplars.technique: Use a tail-truncated doubly robust expansion with explicit weak-overlap moment controls.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the estimator-side decomposition that a uniform WCP envelope must refine into a training-conditional length bound.

method_exemplars.technique: Use a class-level representer and lower-bound formulation for debiased linear functionals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A density-ratio WCP score is likewise a tail-diverging representer, so the result must be stated at the class rather than law-specific level.

open_problems.open_problem: Under a bounded departure from the stochastic-intervention density-ratio model, derive sharp partially identified conformal prediction intervals for Y^g and quantify their unavoidable excess length under heavy overlap tails.

source_refs.claim: Yang et al. introduce semiparametric sensitivity analysis for latent covariate shift and state that further elaboration toward prediction sets for ITE under unmeasured confounding will appear elsewhere.
source_refs.author: Yang, Kuchibhotla, Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Cauchois et al. obtain nearly finite-sample robust predictive coverage over an f-divergence neighborhood of the training distribution, but not sharp causal stochastic-intervention partial identification or heavy-tail length rates.
source_refs.author: Cauchois, Gupta, Ali, Duchi

open_problems.why_unsolved: A misspecified GPS/intervention density ratio breaks the conditional-law invariance behind weighted conformal validity, so the target is no longer point identified by a single reweighting. Existing sensitivity analysis is asymptotic and generic, while divergence-ball robust validation is not tied to causal w=g/pi structure or to the gamma-tail region where an adversary can concentrate distortion. A solution must jointly optimize over admissible target laws and prediction intervals.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identified-set characterization for the target score distribution over a specified density-ratio-departure class, together with finite-sample robust conformal intervals and matching upper and lower excess-length bounds depending on gamma0 and the sensitivity radius.

writing_exemplar.why: It moves from explainable covariate shift to a named latent-shift sensitivity model and identifies a causal prediction-set extension as unfinished.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: causal prediction can be represented as explainable covariate shift -> learned calibration is doubly robust under that invariance -> latent shift breaks invariance and requires a sharp sensitivity/partial-identification interval

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt adversarial f-divergence-ball coverage optimization that converts uncertainty about the target law into a robust conformal calibration constraint.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Both targets require worst-case coverage over a restricted set of shifted laws rather than point identification of one target distribution.

method_exemplars.technique: Use the efficient-influence-function quantile construction and sensitivity-parameter score expansion for endpoint estimation and coverage analysis.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The partial-identification interval endpoints are shifted-law score quantiles with the same nuisance-functional structure.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine whether one can adapt, without knowing gamma0, to the optimal heavy-density-ratio-tail excess-length exponent for valid causal weighted-conformal intervals over a union of tail classes gamma in (1,2).

source_refs.claim: Wang and Goel show that clipping can control expected undercoverage under unbounded or learned ratios by target-level inflation, but do not give a sharp tail-index-adaptive excess-length rate or causal-intervention lower bound.
source_refs.author: Wang, Goel

source_refs.claim: Dorn derives rate-dependent thresholding rules under weak overlap, with the tail regime and nuisance rates entering the clipping choice; no adaptation result for unknown tail behavior is supplied.
source_refs.author: Dorn

source_refs.claim: Qiu et al. show that informative finite-sample/PAC prediction sets are impossible under completely unknown covariate shift and instead obtain asymptotic PAC guarantees, leaving structured-tail adaptation unresolved.
source_refs.author: Qiu, Dobriban, Tchetgen Tchetgen

open_problems.why_unsolved: The clipping level balancing gamma-tail truncation bias and learned-nuisance error depends on gamma, but gamma must be learned from the same rare high-w observations. Completely unknown shift makes finite-sample informative prediction impossible, so any positive adaptive result must exploit nested tail classes and show that tail-index estimation or multiscale clipping preserves training-conditional coverage without an unaccounted adaptation loss.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An adaptive clipped weighted-conformal procedure with a uniform high-probability oracle inequality over gamma in (1,2), together with either a matching adaptive lower bound or a theorem proving no logarithmic adaptation penalty under the stated tail classes.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames the failure of WCP under unbounded learned ratios and makes clipping the central design decision.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: covariate shift needs weighted conformal calibration -> unbounded or learned ratios destabilize the weighted threshold -> the tail index governing optimal clipping is unknown

method_exemplars.technique: Use weak-overlap tail and margin bounds to analyze data-dependent threshold selection and orthogonal nuisance errors.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A clipping selector trades the same rare-region bias against weak-overlap stochastic error as thresholded doubly robust estimation.

method_exemplars.technique: Use its impossibility construction for unknown covariate shift as the boundary condition for what tail-class structure makes adaptation feasible.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The adaptive theorem must avoid the uninformative-set impossibility by exploiting a verifiable density-ratio-tail restriction.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the smallest finite-sample coverage correction, and its length cost, needed when causal weighted conformal prediction uses an estimated stochastic-intervention density ratio rather than the known ratio required by exact weighted exchangeability.

source_refs.claim: Yang et al. prove doubly robust eventual nominal average coverage under explainable covariate shift and explicitly show that informative finite-sample coverage is impossible without prior knowledge of the density ratio.
source_refs.author: Yang, Kuchibhotla, Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Qiu et al. show finite-sample and PAC prediction sets are uninformative under unknown covariate shift, motivating only asymptotic PAC calibration absent structured shift information.
source_refs.author: Qiu, Dobriban, Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Tibshirani et al.'s exact distribution-free result is conditional on a known likelihood ratio; estimation is discussed empirically rather than as a finite-sample learned-weight calibration theorem.
source_refs.author: Tibshirani, Barber, Candès, Ramdas

open_problems.why_unsolved: There is a sharp divide between exact WCP, which requires the ratio, and unknown-shift work, which gets asymptotic calibration or uninformative finite-sample sets. A stochastic intervention supplies additional structure—w=g/pi together with a GPS rate and a tail class—but it remains necessary to prove exactly how that structure yields an informative finite-sample or training-conditional correction when high weights have infinite variance.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-sample or high-probability theorem giving an explicit coverage-inflation functional of GPS error and the gamma-tail envelope, plus a matching lower bound showing that a smaller correction permits undercoverage or forces noninformative intervals.

writing_exemplar.why: Its motivation starts from the precise obstruction—unknown shift makes finite-sample informative prediction impossible—and asks what structure restores useful calibration.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: ordinary conformal validity relies on exchangeability -> unknown covariate shift makes finite-sample informative prediction impossible -> causal density-ratio tails and GPS structure may quantify the escape from this impossibility

method_exemplars.technique: Start from the weighted-exchangeability proof and perturb the weighted empirical score distribution by learned-ratio error.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the exact known-w benchmark whose perturbation determines the minimal calibration correction.

method_exemplars.technique: Use efficient-influence-function quantile calibration and product-bias control to make the learned weight and outcome nuisance remainder orthogonal.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target correction is a score-quantile functional under a shifted causal law with the two nuisance components addressed by doubly robust calibration.

literature_map:
Tibshirani, Barber, Candès and Ramdas (2019, NeurIPS, arXiv:1904.06019) establish exact weighted-conformal coverage under a known likelihood ratio via weighted exchangeability.
Lei and Candès (2021, JRSSB, doi:10.1111/rssb.12459) formulate counterfactual prediction as covariate shift and use weighted conformal intervals; their ITE oracle length is unattainable without assumptions on the joint potential-outcome law.
Qiu, Dobriban and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023, JRSSB, arXiv:2203.06126) prove unknown-shift finite-sample/PAC prediction sets uninformative, replacing them with asymptotic PAC calibration.
Yang, Kuchibhotla and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, JRSSB, arXiv:2203.01761) use efficient influence functions for doubly robust asymptotic calibration under explainable covariate shift and introduce latent-shift sensitivity analysis.
Cauchois, Gupta, Ali and Duchi (2020, arXiv:2008.04267) give finite-sample robust predictive validity over f-divergence neighborhoods, but not causal tail-index efficiency.
Verhaeghe, Jonkers and Van Hoecke (2024/2026, arXiv:2409.20412) frame continuous dose-response conformal prediction as covariate shift and approximate local treatment coverage with kernel weighting.
Schröder et al. (2025, NeurIPS, arXiv:2407.03094) provide finite-sample conformal intervals for continuous-treatment potential outcomes when propensity scores are estimated; they report wider intervals in sparsely observed treatment regions but no rate or converse.
Dorn (2025, arXiv:2504.13273) gives weak-overlap thresholded doubly robust t-statistics and identifies the clipping/nuisance-rate cost when the semiparametric bound is infinite, but treats scalar estimation rather than distribution-free prediction sets.
Kiyani, Pappas and Hassani (2024, NeurIPS, arXiv:2406.18814) develop length-optimized conformal sets with infinite-sample duality and finite-sample validity under covariate-shift classes, but no learned heavy-ratio causal frontier.
Wang and Goel (2026, arXiv:2605.02072) analyze clipping for unbounded learned density ratios in conformal inference, obtaining bounded expected undercoverage after target inflation rather than a sharp causal excess-length lower/upper pair.
The live tension is exact known-weight distribution-free validity versus learned-weight asymptotic or robust calibration, compounded by weak-overlap heavy tails; no located paper supplies a matched interval-length exponent and Le Cam converse for stochastic interventions.
A second boundary is complete unknown-shift impossibility versus structured-tail adaptation: the literature does not characterize whether the tail index can be learned without sacrificing honest causal conformal efficiency.
A third boundary is point identification under explainable shift versus sensitivity/partial identification under latent shift, especially when the adversarial discrepancy can concentrate in the high-density-ratio region.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined the closest archived overlap-rate program, stat_ate_overlap_decay/v1, in failed and delaundered-bank forms.
Its README identifies the unresolved blocker as a class-uniform envelope/surrogate comparison plus a shell-matching minimax converse.
review_decision.json records that the claimed sharp frontier had only a root-n lower floor away from the root-n regime.
Inspected rejected angle0 and angle1 proposals: both explicitly mark tail-localized lower bounds as NOT YET PROVED.
Their §11 scope flags withdraw unknown-tail adaptivity and uniform confidence intervals at the critical kappa=1 boundary.
Also inspected adjacent overlap/minimax bank entries; none supplied a closer reviewer-prescribed conformal interval gap.
